data.title: Amtrak's NextGen Acela high-speed train debuts on August 28, connecting Boston to Washington, DC. #EastCoastTravel
data.description: A state-of-the-art high-speed rail service is set to revolutionize travel between Boston and Washington, DC. Commencing on Thursday, August 28, 2025, passengers will have the opportunity to experience the NextGen Acela by Amtrak – a cutting-edge train service that promises to transform travel along the East Coast. This innovative transportation system will significantly reduce travel times, making it the preferred choice for commuters and travelers alike. This development marks a milestone in transportation, showcasing the commitment to enhancing connectivity and efficiency in the region.

data.title: Over 400 global buyers and investors to attend Costa Rica Trade & Investment Summit, with participation of 1,000 companies.
data.description: The upcoming Costa Rica Trade & Investment Summit is set to gather over 1,000 companies, establishing the country as a key hub for trade and investment. For further details and to register, visit www.costaricasummit.com. Taking place in SAN JOSÉ, Costa Rica, the event kicks off on September 1, 2025.

data.title: Executive order bans debanking of crypto industry post termination of Operation Chokepoint 2.0, per White House.
data.description: The White House has announced the conclusion of Operation Chokepoint 2.0, stating that the initiative has been terminated. The administration argued that the operation had been unjust towards the digital assets sector.

data.title: Trade Desk reported 19% revenue growth in Q2 FY2025 with strong focus on CTV, partnerships. Key innovations include Cokai platform upgrade, OpenPath for efficient supply chain, and focus on objectivity in ad tech ecosystem. CEO Jeff Green highlighted the company's vision to buy the entire open Internet objectively for buyers. Company strengthens leadership team with new COO and CFO appointments. Record amount of spend influenced by retail data in Q2. Shares vision to leverage AI, data for improved ad buying performance.
data.description: The Trade Desk, Inc. (NASDAQ: TTD) reported its Q2 2025 financial results, showcasing a 19% revenue growth year-over-year. Notably, Connected TV (CTV) is the fastest-growing channel, with partnerships with major companies like Disney, NBCU, Netflix, and Walmart deepening. The company is focusing on digital advertising innovation, with around 3/4 of client spend through Cokai, their new platform for driving precision and relevance in campaigns. The OpenPath feature is improving supply chain efficiency, with positive results for publishers and clients. The company is aiming for objectivity in ad tech, emphasizing the power of the premium open Internet. The CEO highlighted significant advancements and partnerships, including the Deal Desk feature and work with retail data to measure campaign outcomes accurately. The company is expanding its leadership team, with a new CFO and board member. They aim to capture the trillion-dollar ad industry and leverage AI for more efficient media buying. The company's vision is to buy the entire open Internet objectively for all types of buyers. With a focus on AI-driven strategies, The Trade Desk is poised for accelerated growth in the ad tech industry.

data.title: Mount Logan Capital Inc. reported $8.4M FRE in Q2 2025, +28% YoY, with $4.6M SRE; declared C$0.02/share dividend in line with 24th consecutive quarter and set special meeting for Business Combination on August 22nd. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
data.description: Mount Logan Capital Inc. announced its financial results for the quarter ending June 30, 2025. The asset management segment generated $8.4 million in Fee Related Earnings (FRE) for the trailing twelve months, marking a 28% increase from the previous year. Additionally, the segment produced $4.6 million in Spread Related Earnings (SRE) during the same period. The company declared a quarterly distribution of C$0.02 per common share and filed definitive proxy materials for a proposed Business Combination with 180 Degree Capital. The insurance segment's total assets managed by Mount Logan increased to $680 million, and its total investment assets remained at approximately $1.1 billion. Mount Logan's special meeting of shareholders is scheduled for August 22, 2025, to consider resolutions related to the Business Combination. Total Capital was $142.0 million at June 30, 2025. Consolidated net income before taxes for the second quarter of 2025 was $(3.3) million. Adjusted basic EPS was $0.03 for the second quarter of 2025. The asset management segment's total revenue was $4.5 million, while the FRE was $1.9 million for the quarter. The insurance segment's net income was $3.5 million, although it incurred a loss for the quarter. Mount Logan stated that its fee-based business model remains robust, with a focus on high-quality, private credit assets. The transformative Business Combination with 180 Degree Capital is expected to drive growth in fee and spread-related earnings.
